risk_title: Political and Governance Instability in Merged Areas
risk_summary: The project operates in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa's Merged Areas (former Frontier Regions), which have historically experienced governance fragility and political instability. The Systematic Operations Risk-Rating Tool maintains a 'Substantial' rating for Political and Governance risk across all review periods, indicating persistent structural governance challenges affecting project implementation.
evidence_quote: Political and Governance Substantial Substantial Substantial [Risk Category Rating at Approval, Last Approved Rating, Proposed Rating]

risk_title: Institutional Capacity Constraints in Conflict-Affected Merged Areas
risk_summary: The project targets institutional development in Merged Areas that have experienced prolonged conflict and administrative fragmentation. The 'Substantial' institutional capacity rating reflects ongoing challenges in building functional state institutions in these fragile governance contexts, with zero progress on district administrative centers and sub-district service centers as of September 2025.
evidence_quote: Institutional Capacity for Implementation and Sustainability Substantial Substantial Substantial [Risk Category Rating]; District administrative centers established and functional (Number) 0.00 15-Sep-2025 with target 8.00 Jun/2029

risk_title: Delayed Implementation in Conflict-Sensitive Merged Areas
risk_summary: Despite project effectiveness in September 2023, core institutional development activities have not commenced on-ground implementation. The project shows zero progress on institutional indicators (village council plans, women's committees, administrative service centers) 24 months into implementation, suggesting implementation delays linked to governance and security challenges in the Merged Areas.
evidence_quote: Activities under the Multisectoral Investments and Improved Service Delivery and Institutional Development of Merged Areas components led by the Public Health and Engineering, Local Government, and Communication and Works Departments are expected to commence on-the-ground implementation over the next six months. [as of October 2025, 24 months post-effectiveness]
